signals.reasoning: Cover the live MON short immediately. The trade has gone from a meaningful winner to a live loss, the giveback warning is urgent, and the board now leans bullish rather than bearish.
BTC remains steady but the active veto keeps benchmark exposure in watch mode rather than action mode.
ETH is constructive but not enough to justify fresh risk under the event brake.
SOL has mild relative strength, but the setup is not strong enough to overcome the veto state.
COMP is one of the strongest squeeze-style longs on the sheet, but veto should keep even high-quality continuation names in watch mode for now.
ZEREBRO lacks both clear directional edge and enough execution quality to justify action here.
DASH also has a strong long profile, but the event brake should keep it out of the buy bucket despite the attractive mechanics.
DYM remains the cleanest bearish name on the rest of the board because price is weak and the mechanical bias is firmly negative, though conviction is still capped by the veto regime.
CHILLGUY remains extended and crowded, but veto means this is watch-only rather than an active fade.
MERL has good momentum but not enough structural edge or liquidity quality to justify action during the veto window.
